chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI SMA No.431 of 2018 Date Order with signature of Judge BEFORE: Mr. Justice Arshad Hussain Khan 1. Ghazala Saleem 2. Aslam Pervaiz............................. Petitioners Saleem Ahmed............................... Deceased 25.04.2019 Mr. Farooq Akhtar Shaikh advocate for the petitioner ----------------------------------------------------- ARSHAD HUSSAIN KHAN, J: The petitioners through instant SMA seek grant of letters of administration in respect of immovable property, left by deceased Saleem Ahmed son of Noor Ahmed, who died intestate on 29.06.2018 at Karachi leaving behind following legal heirs:- Sr. # Name of legal heirs Relationship with deceased 01 Mst. Ghazala Saleem (petitioner Nol) Widow 02 Ms. Iqra Saleem Daughter 03 Mst. Tahira Rafi Sister 04 Mst. Shahnaz Saleem Sister 05 Mst. Noor Jahan Sister 06 Mst. Saira Mohsin Sister 07 Mst. Shaheena Sabir Sister 08 Imran Asim Brother 2. Since the deceased did not have any male issue, therefore, along with his widow and daughter the deceased`s brother and sisters have also been arrayed in the list of legal heirs, as mentioned above. Per learned counsel, inadvertently the name of petitioner No.2 could not be mentioned in the list of legal heirs annexed to the instant petitioner. 3. The legal heir at Sr. No.1 is petitioner No.1 also widow of deceased, and said Aslam Pervaiz son of Noor Ahmed is petitioner No.2 and brother of deceased. The legal heirs No.2 is daughter, whereas legal heirs No.3 to 8 are sisters and brother of deceased. The legal heirs No.3,4 & 8 have executed their power of attorneys in favour of the petitioner in respect of grant of instant SMA.

The legal heirs No.2 is daughter, whereas legal heirs No.3 to 8 are sisters and brother of deceased. The legal heirs No.3,4 & 8 have executed their power of attorneys in favour of the petitioner in respect of grant of instant SMA. While, legal heirs No.2, 5, 6 & 7 have sown their affidavit of “No objection” in favour of the petitioners. 3. The affidavits of two independent witnesses namely; Muhammad Sohail son of Muhammad Suleman and Aziz-ur-Rehman son of Abdul Rehman are also available on the record, which support the case of the petitioner. 4. The deceased at the time of his death left behind immovable property, detail whereof is mentioned in the Schedule of Property (at Page 31). Record also shows that the publication of the main petition has been affected in Daily Jang Karachi dated 26.12.2018 but none has appeared. Hence, the matter emerged as non-contentious one, therefore, in my opinion there is no legal impediment to grant the present SMA. Accordingly, this SMA is allowed as per Rules. However, since this matter is non-contentious one, therefore, the petitioners would be at liberty to deposit the original title documents of the immoveable property of the subject proceedings with the Nazir of this Court as surety as well as execute personal bond in the like amount for issuance of letter of administration. S.M.A No.431 of 2018 is disposed of in above terms. JUDGE MUSHARRAF ALI
